title: hieracium alpinum subsp. comptellum
intro: Hieracium alpinum subsp. comptellum, also known as Alpine Hawkweed, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with a history of use in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant effects, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Hieracium alpinum subsp. comptellum are typically made from the plant's leaves, stems, and flowers, and can be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical treatment.
